item.title: Kaine says guardrails on Pentagon firings could see bipartisan support
item.description: Sen. Tim Kaine said guardrails on Pentagon firings could see bipartisan support in Congress, following a string of high-level officers exiting the military during the second Trump administration.

item.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy accuses RFK Jr. of building public health "upon a foundation of lies"
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy, who delivered the key vote to advance Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s nomination, told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that "if you build public health upon a foundation of lies, then you're going to have the absence of adequate public health."

item.title: Transcript: Sen. Tim Kaine on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 28, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of an interview with Sen. Tim Kaine, Democrat of Virginia, that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on June 28, 2026.

item.title: Jan Crawford says remaining Supreme Court decisions could be a "mixed bag" for Trump
item.description: Jan Crawford, CBS News' chief legal correspondent, says that she believes the remaining Supreme Court decisions will be a "mixed bag" for President Trump.

item.title: Face the Nation: Kaine, Cassidy, Crawford
item.description: Missed the second half of the show? Sens. Tim Kaine and Bill Cassidy and CBS News chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford join.

item.title: Sen. Tim Kaine says guardrails on Pentagon firings could see bipartisan support in Congress
item.description: Democratic Sen. Tim Kaine of Virginia told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that he believes there could be bipartisan support for guardrails on Pentagon firings in the aftermath of the departure of Gen. Chris Donahue, the commander of U.S. Army Europe and Africa.

item.title: 5 nurses among 11 killed in French skydiving plane crash
item.description: Mayor Mathieu Klein said the victims "died in full view of their loved ones, who were preparing to film the tandem skydives."

item.title: Douglas Brinkley on America at 250: History tells us hoping for unity is not futile
item.description: In these fiercely-polarized times, the presidential historian reminds us that Americans' freedom has been tested – and has survived – much worse.

item.title: Larry David on "Life, Larry, and the Pursuit of Unhappiness"
item.description: The comic icon behind "Curb Your Enthusiasm" brings his own perspective to America's storied past in a new HBO sketch comedy series – finally making use of his history major from college.

item.title: Iran launches drone, missile attacks targeting Bahrain, Kuwait
item.description: The strikes in Kuwait and Bahrain came after the US and Iran traded attacks over the weekend.

item.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy says "I don't know" if Trump understands the Senate is a separate body
item.description: Republican Sen. Bill Cassidy, who recently lost the primary in his state to a challenger endorsed by President Trump, told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that he thinks Congress will continue to hold Mr. Trump accountable after he and Sens. Thom Tillis and John Conryn leave, but he added "I don't know" if the president knows the Senate is a separate body from the presidency.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (June 28)
item.description: Jane Pauley hosts a special program celebrating our nation's semiquincentennial, "These United States - America at 250."

item.title: Mass graves of dog remains found at Northern California animal rescue
item.description: The Humboldt County Sheriff's Office said they found 600 dog collars in an area where they suspect dozens of dogs were killed.

item.title: Open: This is "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 28, 2026
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," Sen. Bill Cassidy discusses the explosive meeting between President Trump and Senate Republicans as well as why he changed his vote on the Iran war powers resolution. Plus, Sen. Tim Kaine and CBS News chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford join.

item.title: The "Sunday Morning" Essential American Songbook: Defining America through its music
item.description: As our nation celebrates its 250th birthday, "Sunday Morning" asked some notable Americans to name their favorite songs by American artists. The result: A diverse list of 250 songs from all musical genres that reflects a beautiful representation of America itself.

item.title: Nature: Bald eagles
item.description: We leave you this Sunday with an enduring symbol of our United States: the bald eagle, first seen on the Great Seal of the United States in 1782. Videographer: Carl Mrozek.

item.title: 6/28: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," Sen. Bill Cassidy discusses the explosive meeting between President Trump and Senate Republicans as well as why he changed his vote on the Iran war powers resolution. Plus, Sen. Tim Kaine and CBS News chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford join.

item.title: Israel moves to formally recognize Armenian WWI deaths as a genocide
item.description: The step, which still needs approval in Parliament, reflects deteriorating ties between Israel and Turkey.

item.title: Douglas Brinkley on America at 250: History tells us hoping for unity is not futile
item.description: In these fiercely-polarized times, the presidential historian reminds us that Americans' freedom has been tested – and has survived – much worse.

item.title: Songbook: James Taylor sings "Moon River"
item.description: Six-time Grammy-winning singer-songwriter James Taylor, whose choices of essential American songs include the 1961 hit "Moon River," performs Henry Mancini's tender song of heartbreak for "Sunday Morning" viewers. Accompanying Taylor are Kevin Hays on keyboards, Jon Suters on bass, and Nick Halley on percussion. [Check out the complete "Sunday Morning" Essential American Songbook at cbsnews.com/songbook.]

item.title: American Panorama: From sea to shining sea
item.description: "Sunday Morning" presents views of America the Beautiful. Photographs by Jonathan Irish.

item.title: Photography and the secret of Frederick Douglass' power
item.description: Frederick Douglass, who was born into slavery and became an influential orator, writer and intellectual, was the most photographed person in America in the 19th century. Nancy Giles explores how Douglass used the early photographic medium to promote the cause of abolition.

item.title: Extended interview: Larry David
item.description: In this web exclusive, Larry David talks with longtime friend and collaborator Susie Essman about his new HBO sketch comedy series, "Life, Larry, and the Pursuit of Unhappiness."

item.title: Videos show U.S. rescue team pull mother, baby from rubble in Venezuela
item.description: Hundreds of U.S. search and rescue workers are on the ground in Venezuela after deadly quakes struck the South American nation.

item.title: Larry David on "Life, Larry, and the Pursuit of Unhappiness"
item.description: Larry David brings his own comic perspective to America's storied history in the new HBO sketch comedy series, "Life, Larry, and the Pursuit of Unhappiness." He talks with Susie Essman about finally making use of his history major from college, and how he took comments from one of the show's producers, former President Barack Obama.

item.title: Celebrating Fourth of July fireworks
item.description: Fireworks were invented in China, and perfected in Italy. But it was immigrants that brought their pyrotechnical secrets to America, creating a Fourth of July tradition: ever-grander fireworks displays. Correspondent Faith Salie visits the New Castle, Pa., fireworks company Pyrotechnico, which will be creating a world-record-worthy Independence Day celebration in Washington, D.C. this July 4th.

item.title: Governors Island: An oasis in New York Harbor
item.description: One of New York City's most unique public spaces is a 172-acre island off the tip of Manhattan. Jane Pauley offers a tour of Governors Island, celebrated for its parks, cultural events, and sweeping harbor views.

item.title: NASA prepares to launch rescue to save telescope from falling to Earth
item.description: The $30 million salvage operation gets underway as soon as this week with the planned launch of a robotic lifesaver.

item.title: 6/28: Sunday Morning
item.description: Host Jane Pauley celebrates America at 250. Featured: The Founding Fathers; driving historic Route 66; the Essential American Songbook; a melting pot of cuisine; the ERA; the transcontinental railroad; fireworks; and Larry David's skewed take on American history.

item.title: Trump-endorsed Julia Letlow projected to win Louisiana GOP Senate runoff
item.description: Rep. Julia Letlow was endorsed by President Trump in the state's Republican Senate runoff to replace Sen. Bill Cassidy, who did not receive enough votes in the primary to advance.
